target_definition: Binary default flag derived from 'loan_status'. BAD (1): Charged Off, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Charged Off, Default. GOOD (0): Fully Paid, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Fully Paid. Default rate: 19.61%. Excluded indeterminate: 88,426 rows (45.9%). Final sample: 104,164 observations.

data_dictionary.acc_now_delinq: The number of accounts on which the borrower is now delinquent.
data_dictionary.acc_open_past_24mths: Number of trades opened in past 24 months.
data_dictionary.addr_state: The state provided by the borrower in the loan application
data_dictionary.all_util: Balance to credit limit on all trades
data_dictionary.annual_inc: The self-reported annual income provided by the borrower during registration.
data_dictionary.annual_inc_joint: The combined self-reported annual income provided by the co-borrowers during registration
data_dictionary.application_type: Indicates whether the loan is an individual application or a joint application with two co-borrowers
data_dictionary.avg_cur_bal: Average current balance of all accounts
data_dictionary.bc_open_to_buy: Total open to buy on revolving bankcards.
data_dictionary.bc_util: Ratio of total current balance to high credit/credit limit for all bankcard accounts.
data_dictionary.collections_12_mths_ex_med: Number of collections in 12 months excluding medical collections
data_dictionary.delinq_2yrs: The number of 30+ days past-due incidences of delinquency in the borrower's credit file for the past 2 years
data_dictionary.delinq_amnt: The past-due amount owed for the accounts on which the borrower is now delinquent.
data_dictionary.desc: Loan description provided by the borrower
data_dictionary.dti: A ratio calculated using the borrower’s total monthly debt payments on the total debt obligations, excluding mortgage and the requested LC loan, divided by the borrower’s self-reported monthly income.
data_dictionary.dti_joint: A ratio calculated using the co-borrowers' total monthly payments on the total debt obligations, excluding mortgages and the requested LC loan, divided by the co-borrowers' combined self-reported monthly income
data_dictionary.earliest_cr_line: The month the borrower's earliest reported credit line was opened
data_dictionary.emp_length: Employment length in years. Possible values are between 0 and 10 where 0 means less than one year and 10 means ten or more years.
data_dictionary.emp_title: The job title supplied by the Borrower when applying for the loan.*
data_dictionary.fico_range_high: The upper boundary range the borrower’s FICO at loan origination belongs to.
data_dictionary.fico_range_low: The lower boundary range the borrower’s FICO at loan origination belongs to.
data_dictionary.funded_amnt: The total amount committed to that loan at that point in time.
data_dictionary.funded_amnt_inv: The total amount committed by investors for that loan at that point in time.
data_dictionary.home_ownership: The home ownership status provided by the borrower during registration or obtained from the credit report. Our values are: RENT, OWN, MORTGAGE, OTHER
data_dictionary.id: A unique LC assigned ID for the loan listing.
data_dictionary.il_util: Ratio of total current balance to high credit/credit limit on all install acct
data_dictionary.initial_list_status: The initial listing status of the loan. Possible values are – W, F
data_dictionary.inq_last_12m: Number of credit inquiries in past 12 months
data_dictionary.inq_last_6mths: The number of inquiries in past 6 months (excluding auto and mortgage inquiries)
data_dictionary.installment: The monthly payment owed by the borrower if the loan originates.
data_dictionary.issue_d: The month which the loan was funded
data_dictionary.last_credit_pull_d: The most recent month LC pulled credit for this loan
data_dictionary.last_fico_range_high: The upper boundary range the borrower’s last FICO pulled belongs to.
data_dictionary.last_fico_range_low: The lower boundary range the borrower’s last FICO pulled belongs to.
data_dictionary.loan_amnt: The listed amount of the loan applied for by the borrower. If at some point in time, the credit department reduces the loan amount, then it will be reflected in this value.
data_dictionary.max_bal_bc: Maximum current balance owed on all revolving accounts
data_dictionary.member_id: A unique LC assigned Id for the borrower member.
data_dictionary.mo_sin_old_il_acct: Months since oldest bank installment account opened
data_dictionary.mo_sin_old_rev_tl_op: Months since oldest revolving account opened
data_dictionary.mo_sin_rcnt_rev_tl_op: Months since most recent revolving account opened
data_dictionary.mo_sin_rcnt_tl: Months since most recent account opened
data_dictionary.mths_since_last_delinq: The number of months since the borrower's last delinquency.
data_dictionary.mths_since_last_major_derog: Months since most recent 90-day or worse rating
data_dictionary.mths_since_last_record: The number of months since the last public record.
data_dictionary.mths_since_rcnt_il: Months since most recent installment accounts opened
data_dictionary.mths_since_recent_bc: Months since most recent bankcard account opened.
data_dictionary.mths_since_recent_bc_dlq: Months since most recent bankcard delinquency
data_dictionary.mths_since_recent_revol_delinq: Months since most recent revolving delinquency.
data_dictionary.num_accts_ever_120_pd: Number of accounts ever 120 or more days past due
data_dictionary.num_actv_bc_tl: Number of currently active bankcard accounts
data_dictionary.num_actv_rev_tl: Number of currently active revolving trades
data_dictionary.num_rev_tl_bal_gt_0: Number of revolving trades with balance >0
data_dictionary.num_tl_120dpd_2m: Number of accounts currently 120 days past due (updated in past 2 months)
data_dictionary.num_tl_30dpd: Number of accounts currently 30 days past due (updated in past 2 months)
data_dictionary.num_tl_90g_dpd_24m: Number of accounts 90 or more days past due in last 24 months
data_dictionary.num_tl_op_past_12m: Number of accounts opened in past 12 months
data_dictionary.open_acc: The number of open credit lines in the borrower's credit file.
data_dictionary.open_acc_6m: Number of open trades in last 6 months
data_dictionary.open_il_12m: Number of installment accounts opened in past 12 months
data_dictionary.open_il_24m: Number of installment accounts opened in past 24 months
data_dictionary.open_act_il: Number of currently active installment trades
data_dictionary.open_rv_12m: Number of revolving trades opened in past 12 months
data_dictionary.open_rv_24m: Number of revolving trades opened in past 24 months
data_dictionary.out_prncp: Remaining outstanding principal for total amount funded
data_dictionary.out_prncp_inv: Remaining outstanding principal for portion of total amount funded by investors
data_dictionary.percent_bc_gt_75: Percentage of all bankcard accounts > 75% of limit.
data_dictionary.policy_code:
publicly available policy_code=1
new products not publicly available policy_code=2
data_dictionary.purpose: A category provided by the borrower for the loan request.
data_dictionary.pymnt_plan: Indicates if a payment plan has been put in place for the loan
data_dictionary.revol_util: Revolving line utilization rate, or the amount of credit the borrower is using relative to all available revolving credit.
data_dictionary.term: The number of payments on the loan. Values are in months and can be either 36 or 60.
data_dictionary.title: The loan title provided by the borrower
data_dictionary.tot_cur_bal: Total current balance of all accounts
data_dictionary.total_acc: The total number of credit lines currently in the borrower's credit file
data_dictionary.total_bal_il: Total current balance of all installment accounts
data_dictionary.total_pymnt: Payments received to date for total amount funded
data_dictionary.total_pymnt_inv: Payments received to date for portion of total amount funded by investors
data_dictionary.url: URL for the LC page with listing data.
data_dictionary.verification_status: Indicates if income was verified by LC, not verified, or if the income source was verified
data_dictionary.verified_status_joint: Indicates if the co-borrowers' joint income was verified by LC, not verified, or if the income source was verified
data_dictionary.zip_code: The first 3 numbers of the zip code provided by the borrower in the loan application.
data_dictionary.revol_bal_joint: Sum of revolving credit balance of the co-borrowers, net of duplicate balances
data_dictionary.sec_app_fico_range_low: FICO range (high) for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_fico_range_high: FICO range (low) for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_earliest_cr_line: Earliest credit line at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_inq_last_6mths: Credit inquiries in the last 6 months at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_mort_acc: Number of mortgage accounts at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_open_acc: Number of open trades at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_revol_util: Ratio of total current balance to high credit/credit limit for all revolving accounts
data_dictionary.sec_app_open_act_il: Number of currently active installment trades at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_num_rev_accts: Number of revolving accounts at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_chargeoff_within_12_mths: Number of charge-offs within last 12 months at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_collections_12_mths_ex_med: Number of collections within last 12 months excluding medical collections at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.sec_app_mths_since_last_major_derog: Months since most recent 90-day or worse rating at time of application for the secondary applicant
data_dictionary.hardship_flag: Flags whether or not the borrower is on a hardship plan
data_dictionary.hardship_reason: Describes the reason the hardship plan was offered
data_dictionary.hardship_status: Describes if the hardship plan is active, pending, canceled, completed, or broken
data_dictionary.deferral_term: Amount of months that the borrower is expected to pay less than the contractual monthly payment amount due to a hardship plan
data_dictionary.hardship_amount: The interest payment that the borrower has committed to make each month while they are on a hardship plan
data_dictionary.hardship_start_date: The start date of the hardship plan period
data_dictionary.hardship_end_date: The end date of the hardship plan period
data_dictionary.payment_plan_start_date: The day the first hardship plan payment is due. For example, if a borrower has a hardship plan period of 3 months, the start date is the start of the three-month period in which the borrower is allowed to make interest-only payments.
data_dictionary.hardship_length: The number of months the borrower will make smaller payments than normally obligated due to a hardship plan
data_dictionary.hardship_dpd: Account days past due as of the hardship plan start date
data_dictionary.hardship_loan_status: Loan Status as of the hardship plan start date
data_dictionary.orig_projected_additional_accrued_intere: The original projected additional interest amount that will accrue for the given hardship payment plan as of the Hardship Start Date. This field will be null if the borrower has broken their hardship payment plan.
data_dictionary.hardship_payoff_balance_amount: The payoff balance amount as of the hardship plan start date
data_dictionary.hardship_last_payment_amount: The last payment amount as of the hardship plan start date
data_dictionary.disbursement_method: The method by which the borrower receives their loan. Possible values are: CASH, DIRECT_PAY
data_dictionary.debt_settlement_flag: Flags whether or not the borrower, who has charged-off, is working with a debt-settlement company.
data_dictionary.debt_settlement_flag_date: The most recent date that the Debt_Settlement_Flag has been set
data_dictionary.settlement_status: The status of the borrower’s settlement plan. Possible values are: COMPLETE, ACTIVE, BROKEN, CANCELLED, DENIED, DRAFT
data_dictionary.settlement_date: The date that the borrower agrees to the settlement plan
data_dictionary.settlement_amount: The loan amount that the borrower has agreed to settle for
data_dictionary.settlement_percentage: The settlement amount as a percentage of the payoff balance amount on the loan
data_dictionary.settlement_term: The number of months that the borrower will be on the settlement plan

consistency_checks.Description: Funded amount cannot exceed loan amount
Interest rate must be a valid percentage
Revolving utilisation must be a plausible percentage

consistency_summary.note: 7 of 7 possible rules applicable to this dataset (some columns may not exist in Dataset 2)

feature_log.rationale: Credit history length — longer history generally predicts lower default
Loan vintage — controls for origination cohort effects
LC-assigned credit grade — strong proxy for creditworthiness (1=best)
Finer-grained credit grade — captures risk within a grade bucket
Interest rate — higher rates indicate riskier borrowers
60-month flag — longer term loans have higher cumulative default probability
Monthly payment burden as share of income — affordability indicator
Loan-to-income ratio — key affordability measure for unsecured lending
Employment stability — longer tenure predicts lower default; -1 = unknown
Housing tenure flags — mortgage holders tend to be lower risk
Any 30+ dpd in last 2 years — strong default predictor
Delinquency within last 12 months — recency amplifies risk signal
Any derogatory public record — bankruptcy or collection flag
High inquiry count signals credit-seeking behaviour / financial stress
Proportion of accounts still open — portfolio activity indicator

split_details.note: OOT=latest 20% by issue_year. Train/Test=random stratified 70/30 within Dev 80%

kpi_scoreboard.Formula: Count deciles where bad rate rises vs adjacent higher-risk decile

confusion_matrix.note: 0.5 threshold used for reporting only — NOT necessarily the correct business decision threshold. Optimal threshold should be set based on false-negative/false-positive cost ratio.

DataUnderstandingAgent.reasoning: Target auto-detected as 'loan_status'. Values classified dynamically: BAD→1, GOOD→0, INDETERMINATE→excluded. Behavioral leakage scan ran univariate AUC on all numeric columns; 1 columns scored ≥ 0.65 and should be reviewed.
recommendations: Confirm target definition with business stakeholders before modelling
Review leakage column list — ensure all post-origination fields are excluded
Investigate 1 behavioral leakage candidates before feature engineering
Review 8 identifier columns — verify none carry signal
Investigate date columns for credit age feature derivation

DQRAgent.reasoning: Missing values above 40% threshold flagged for exclusion as they provide insufficient information for reliable imputation. IQR and z-score methods applied for outlier detection. Duplicates checked on loan ID and member ID.
recommendations: Drop or carefully impute 20 high-missing columns
Apply median imputation for numeric features with <40% missing
Apply mode imputation for categorical features
Review flagged outliers — clip or winsorise extreme values before modelling

observations: Date-derived features: credit age, months since earliest credit line
Domain features: DTI enrichment, revolving utilisation, delinquency indicators
Encoding: ordinal grade/subgrade, WOE for categorical variables
Imputation: median for numeric, mode for categorical
FeatureEngineeringAgent.reasoning: Domain-driven features created based on credit risk best practices. Grade and subgrade encoded ordinally to preserve risk ordering. WOE encoding applied to categorical variables to capture default rate signal. Post-origination raw columns dropped after deriving engineered equivalents.
recommendations: Review feature_log for any domain features that may be controversial
Validate WOE bins are monotonic for scorecarding compliance
Check that no engineered feature introduces inadvertent leakage
Consider adding vintage features if origination date is available

observations: Top features by IV: int_rate_clean (IV=0.4014), subgrade_ordinal (IV=0.3938), grade_ordinal (IV=0.3276)
VariableSelectionAgent.reasoning: IV threshold 0.02 applied — features below this have insufficient discriminatory power for credit risk modelling. High correlation pairs resolved by keeping the feature with higher IV. Final shortlist capped at 25 features to prevent overfitting.
recommendations: Review rejected features — some may have business value not captured by IV
Validate WOE bins for monotonicity before scorecard deployment
Consider interaction features between top IV variables
Ensure all 9 selected features pass regulatory scrutiny

observations: Models trained: LogisticRegression, RandomForest, XGBoost, LightGBM, GradientBoosting_AutoML
ModelDevelopmentAgent.reasoning: Champion selected via AUC on held-out test set with overfit penalty: score = auc_test - max(0, overfit - 0.03) * 2. Penalises models with train-test gap > 3pp to favour generalisable models.
recommendations: Validate champion model on an independent out-of-time sample
Run PSI analysis to assess population stability before deployment
Review hyperparameters for regulatory model documentation
Consider ensemble or stacking if AUC is below 0.70

observations: SHAP values computed for champion model: XGBoost
Top 5 features by mean |SHAP|: int_rate_clean (0.4799), loan_to_income (0.1618), annual_inc (0.1368), dti (0.1117), tot_cur_bal (0.0627)
Adverse action codes generated for 1 sample applicants
ExplainabilityAgent.reasoning: SHAP TreeExplainer used for tree-based models (XGBoost, RandomForest, GradientBoosting); KernelExplainer fallback used for Logistic Regression. Mean absolute SHAP value used as global feature importance metric. Adverse action codes derived from top negative SHAP contributors per applicant.
recommendations: Review top SHAP features for regulatory and fairness concerns
Ensure adverse action codes map to standard industry reason codes
Conduct disparate impact analysis on protected attribute proxies
Document SHAP findings in model risk management documentation

ValidationAgent.reasoning: Industry-standard thresholds applied: AUC ≥ 0.65, KS ≥ 0.20, Gini ≥ 0.30. PSI < 0.10 indicates population stability; 0.10–0.25 moderate shift; >0.25 unstable. Brier score measures calibration quality — lower is better. Challenger comparison run across all trained candidate models.
recommendations: Schedule quarterly PSI monitoring to detect population drift
Re-calibrate model if Brier score degrades by >0.02 from baseline
Conduct annual model validation with independent out-of-time data
Obtain sign-off from model risk governance before production deployment
Document all validation findings in the Model Risk Management (MRM) report

recommendations.title: Target Variable Definition
recommendations.recommendation: Binary default flag derived from 'loan_status'. BAD (1): Charged Off, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Charged Off, Default. GOOD (0): Fully Paid, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Fully Paid. Default rate: 19.61%. Excluded indeterminate: 88,426 rows (45.9%). Final sample: 104,164 observations.
recommendations.rationale: Target auto-detected as 'loan_status'. Dynamic value classification applied: Current→EXCL, Late (31-120 days)→EXCL, In Grace Period→EXCL + 1 more excluded

recommendations.title: Feature Shortlist
recommendations.rationale: IV threshold 0.02 applied. 9 features selected from 49 candidates. High-correlation pairs resolved by retaining higher-IV feature.

recommendations.title: Champion Model Selection
recommendations.rationale: Champion selected via AUC on held-out test set with overfit penalty. AUC=0.7058, overfit delta=0.0121.

recommendations.title: Deployment Readiness
recommendations.rationale: AUC=0.7058. Industry thresholds: AUC≥0.65, KS≥0.20, Gini≥0.30. Validation outcome: PASS.

audit_log.detail: Binary default flag derived from 'loan_status'. BAD (1): Charged Off, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Charged Off, Default. GOOD (0): Fully Paid, Does not meet the credit policy. Status:Fully Paid. Default rate: 19.61%. Excluded indeterminate: 88,426 rows (45.9%). Final sample: 104,164 observations.
